column1: OK, We’ve Done it Again! (Expert Help Extended),"OK, We’ve Done it Again! (Expert Help Extended) Hey Honey! Carson Co-founder of Wealthy Affiliate I hope your first few weeks at Wealthy Affiliate have been an awesome experience for you! The ultimate goal of the service at WA is to make YOUR life much easier as an Internet Entrepreneur, and smooth and simplify the journey to success. We really hope you have gotten a good taste of this! The journey and path to building a thriving business doesn’t need to be a tough one, it is just a matter of taking action on your goals. If you can do that, you WILL achieve success. As you move forward, we want you to CONTINUE to feel at home within the community. Because of this, we are going to extend your “expert support” within Wealthy Affiliate. We want you to take your time, work through the training, get help, learn lots, and ultimately succeed. At the end of the day, we know that you are aware of our Premium and Premium Plus+ services. You can decide when and whether or not you upgrade at some point in the future when you are ready to take your business to the next level. During this extension, you have full access to us directly, as well, you have access to 10,000’s of resident experts that hang out at Wealthy Affiliate on a daily basis and run their successful businesses utilizing our platform. As a reminder, there are several ways in which you can get help as you move forward with your business. Live chat Comments in the training Blogs Profile message directly to owners (Kyle & Carson) As a Premium member, if you do decide to go in that direction at any point (no pressure), I want to remind you that you will have unlimited help within WA, including private support and coaching. This means you can connect with Myself or Carson for private mentoring/coaching, along with any other member within the community. :) We really want to help you get your initial foundation built, and from there you can build a successful and scalable long-term business. The first few weeks and months are critical to your business. If you can get your foundational building blocks in place, the sky is truly the limit within the online world. There is no “cap” to what you can achieve. Anyways, I wanted to deliver this good news to you today. :) We appreciate you and look forward to seeing your progress and succeed as you much forward through your journey within the online business world. Cheers, Carson Co-founder of Wealthy Affiliate WealthyAffiliate.com Follow Kyle and Carson on Instagram Hosting Websites Support Training Domains Privacy Policy Terms of Service Ask a question Manage your email preferences © 2023 WealthyAffiliate.com",1

I’m Natalie, Director of CRO & UX Strategy at The Good. As an end-to-end experience designer, strategy director, and creative leader, I help ecommerce leaders make customer-first decisions that are data-informed.
My goal is to create digital experiences and product journeys that are as delightful, responsive, and welcoming as a greeting from your favorite barista.
If you’re looking for suggested reading to improve your conversion rate today, I recommend you take a look at How To Measure (And Improve) Your CRO Program In Each Stage Of Business.
From startup to enterprise, this article covers how to make sure you have a healthy CRO program at three key business stages.
Check out an overview of our services below. If you have any questions, reply and let us know.

Comprehensive Conversion Audit – Our team of CRO Strategists conduct a thorough audit of your end-to-end customer journey and build a detailed (typically 60-100 page) report outlining your site's most impactful strengths and opportunities.
Conversion Growth Program™ – If you want a “set it and forget it” CRO program, this is the service for you. We handle everything from research to reporting and make you look good in the process.

Conversion Growth Assessment™ – In one week, we audit the most important pages of your store and present a handful of design tweaks you can implement to make meaningful improvements in your conversion rate.

Conversion Growth Accelerator™ – This immersive program is the perfect blend of training, coaching, and community support. We give you everything you need to apply the CRO lessons we’ve learned from our enterprise clients to your own business.
I’ll pass the torch to James for the next email so he can share some thoughts on optimizing your ecommerce website.

2035 NW Front Ave, Suite 202, Portland, OR

Prepare for another lettuce crunch.,"First Up Plus, an exclusive first look inside the Eglinton Crosstown LRT line ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ First Up By Ashley Okwuosa Good morning. After 13 years of rule from the political centre and right, most Torontontians say they are eager to elect a progressive mayor. Here’s the latest. MUST READS Steve Russell/Toronto Star Transportation An exclusive first look inside the Eglinton Crosstown LRT line Three days after the consortium building the LRT sued the provincial agencies in charge, accusing them of taking an “irresponsibly hands-off approach,” that same consortium gave the Star an exclusive interview and tour of the light rail transit line. Until now, journalists and the public have had little insight into where the line’s construction stands since it first began in 2011, or what is holding things up. Read more for exclusive images and videos of the Eglinton Crosstown LRT line and Lex Harvey’s take on the biggest threat to the $12.8-billion transit project. Ken Wolter/Dreamstime FOOD Canada’s food supply is headed for unchartered territory A majority of all fresh fruits and vegetables in Canada is imported, and California supplies a hefty chunk. Most of last year’s 265,000 metric tons of U.S.-imported lettuce came from the state. But last fall, heat, drought and disease coalesced to blight its lettuce crops so severely that the leafy green abruptly vanished from Canadian grocery store shelves for a few weeks. More evidence of California’s climate and water stress can be found across the produce aisle if you know where to look. Kate Allen breaks down what this will mean for our fridges, grocery bills and our health here in Canada. Adrian Wyld/The Canadian Press Federal Politics Justin Trudeau is learning the hard way that keeping Canada competitive comes with a high political price In the mad scramble this week to prevent the Stellantis EV battery plant under construction in Windsor from heading to the U.S., Justin Trudeau has found that it is going to take money, voter and investor confidence, less red tape, reduced regulations and more infrastructure, writes Tonda MacCharles. But how much money can the government afford to shell out? And what kind of precedent will it set for businesses that want to invest in Canada? Here’s what’s at stake in the battle to keep the Stellantis EV battery plant in Canada. Lance McMillan/Toronto Star Workplace equity This CEO reflects on her viral comments that sparked a global conversation about sexism in the workplace After experiencing a series of sexist comments in a very public forum, Amanda Blanc — the first woman CEO at one of the world’s leading insurance companies — fired off a bluntly honest LinkedIn post. Her missive went viral, resonating with female corporates worldwide. A year later, Blanc tells Christine Dobby that women’s leadership in the office remains a significant disappointment. Here’s what she believes needs to be done to address diversity in leadership. Ella Olsson/Unsplash Home economics Families are catering food instead of buying groceries. Is it really cheaper? Most social media hacks come and go like the tide. But one from this January seemingly took hold and left the internet divided. The hack: don’t buy groceries, but order catering to save time and money on meal prep. While some took to social media to proclaim the tip a lifesaver, others were less convinced. Joshua Chong crunched the numbers and spoke with two experts to figure out the lesser of two evils. Need a trusty guide through the housing crunch? Living Here, the Star’s free newsletter, can help. Each week, it’ll bring you the analysis and expert advice you need to navigate renting, buying, owning or just getting by in and around Toronto — plus more of the Star’s essential real estate coverage. Sign up for free here. POV Andrew Phillips: Are politics just a big joke to Pierre Poilievre? Donovan Vincent: Here’s how a lack of disclosure tripped up this opinion column on Toronto traffic. Rick Salutin: In search of passion from candidates at the first Toronto mayoral debate. Edward Keenan: Will Mitzie Hunter’s grand Scarborough subway plans help put her mayoral bid on the right track? Emmy Egulu: Mark Saunders’s troubling legacy and mayoral ambitions. Bob Hepburn: Why Olivia Chow lost the 2014 mayor’s race — and could lose again. VISIT THIS Staff photographer/Toronto Star After traditional chain retailers such as The Gap and Eddie Bauer shuttered or halted expansion plans, Fairview Mall’s leasing agents pivoted to food. What used to be Sears, the mall’s department store anchor, is fast becoming an East Asian food destination. From three new bubble tea shops, two Chinese bakeries and a hand-pulled noodle place, Karon Lui offers a look at how Fairview Mall is leaning into feeding its shoppers. Thanks for reading. You can reach the First Up team at firstup@thestar.ca, and I will see you back here tomorrow. Notificação de Irregularidade no Cadastro de Pessoa Física (CPF)
Regularize a sua situação fiscal vinculada ao seu CPF

Informamos que seu CPF encontra-se em situação IRREGULAR. Devido à não declaração e ao não pagamento de seus impostos, essa pendência gera complicações graves, como bloqueio de contas bancárias, dificuldades na emissão de documentos oficiais, como passaporte, abertura de contas, título de eleitor, entre outros, e o bloqueio em diversas transações administrativas. Além disso, a chave PIX também será suspensa até a regularização da situação.

Para regularizar sua situação, é necessário o pagamento imediato da multa sobre os valores não declarados. Atenção: Se a regularização não for efetuada, o seu CPF será SUSPENSO e quaisquer contas bancárias vinculadas a ele também poderão ser bloqueadas.
Após realizar o pagamento, o sistema levará até 48 horas para atualizar seu status. Evite novos custos ou complicações regularizando a situação no prazo.
ATENÇÃO: Este e-mail foi enviado automaticamente pelo sistema de notificações. Para mais informações, acesse o site oficial ou entre em contato com o atendimento ao cliente.

eBay request: Please follow the Member Verification Procedure (Second Notice),"eBay request: Please follow the Member Verification Procedure (Second Notice) Dear eBay Member, Due to recent account takeovers and unauthorized listings, eBay is introducing a new account verification method. From time to time, randomly selected accounts (seller and/or buyer) are subjected to an advanced verification process based on our merchant accounts/bank relations and customers credit card. eBay may also request in an email message scanned/faxed copies of one or more photo ID's. Your account confirmation may go wrong if your credit card/bank account is expired, or if you have changed your credit card number, billing address etc. without letting us know about the change. Subject of this verification process are also the accounts that have unpaid dues to eBay. Your account is not suspended, but if in 48 hours after you receive this message your account is not confirmed we reserve the right to suspend your eBay registration. If you received this notice and you are not the authorized account holder, please be aware that it is in violation of eBay policy to represent oneself as another eBay user. Such action may also be in violation of local, national, and/or international law. eBay is committed to assist law enforcement with any inquires related to attempts to misappropriate personal information with the intent to commit fraud or theft. Information will be provided at the request of law enforcement agencies to ensure that perpetrators are prosecuted to the full extent of the law. Note: If this is the second time you receive this notice, it might be because you have made a mistake when you entered your details or that the account was not updated at all. To confirm your identity with us click here: We apologize in advance for any inconvenience this may cause you and we would like to thank you for your cooperation as we review this matter. Respectfully, Trust and Safety Department eBay Inc. This eBay notice was sent to you based on your eBay account preferences. If you would like to review your notification preferences for other types of communications, click here. If you would like to receive this email in text only, click here. column1: 07.18.2002 GnomeREPORT Keep your Web site working fast and smart with UltraDNS. More reliableperformance means more traffic, more sales, and more profits. Outsource yourDNS management and save. Basic DNS for only $1.00 a month. Click here tofind out more.I thought I baked my Dell PowerEdge 1400SC server yesterday when it refused to boot completely. I borrowed a data vac from a friend and decided to clean all of the dust and cat hair from my systems, then after reconnecting everything, the server would boot to the initial splash screen, where there is a POST progress bar, but would not proceed past past about the first 1/5th of the bar. Uh-oh. What did I fry?As it turns out, it was another case of ""always check the small stuff"". Oh, I had all of the cables plugged back in to the server properly, I checked all of that, but it didn't dawn on me until about a half dozen boot attempts later that I should probably turn an eye toward the other end of the cables. That server is connected to an 8-port Compaq KVM switch and with all of the yanking, plucking and shuffling, the keyboard cable had been eeked out of the switch just a tad. Why the server didn't display a keyboard warning message like every other computer on the planet, I can only attribute to the BIOS developers. Server Satchel, running Red Hat Linux 7.2 is now operating as it was previously. The penguin is happy. The server case is even black. How appropriate...See You in August,

Finely crafted by Bernie Klinder all of the security features crammed into Windows XP, where does one start? Do you need VPN capability? Is NTFS an appropriate file system choice for you? Is there any value at all in Simple File Sharing? After much hounding, Bernie Klinder has drawn up a healthy checklist of things you need to address under Windows XP in order to maintain a system that is both security and compatible with the rest of your computing world.

GnomeSYSTEMATI Still Makes Video Cards? it isn't so! Yes, apparently they do still have something to say about NVIDIA's dominance in the video card market. Seeing as how they've been all but dumped by the majority of top-tier system makers, it's hard to believe that they are capable of a serious comeback. Judging from what AnandTech has to say about the forthcoming R300-based cards, it appears that they haven't thrown in the towel just yet. I'm all for competition, so I welcome the effective re-entrance into the high end market. Let's all hope that their driver development has improved as much as their hardware seems to have. GnomeASSISTANTTechnical Censorship I liked that cup of Mocha (spelled M-O-C-H-A and I'll explain in a minute) I drank at Starbucks when Jake and I visited last week, but if I wanted to relay that to someone using a Yahoo email account, you might see an alternate word. Thus the additional hyphenated spelling just in case those of you using Yahoo to subscribe to Lockergnome actually received your issues and the word has been altered to suit their personal opinion of my choice in drinks. They've also had a terrible time interpretting our W3C standardized HTML lately, dropping in copious #message references throughout the text in their new Beta email client. So why the text replacement? It's another filtering technique to stop certain words that might relate to JavaScript functions. I guess I'll have to choose another beverage next time. Silly me.

GnomeSTUFFYer Kidding... Really? all sorts of sarcastic today, aren't I? Those of us that have had to manage budgets are used to dealing with math in terms of hardware, software, maintenance and personnel costs. It's not really that difficult. It's certainly not hard to see that dropping a free version of Linux on a server with less hardware requirements just to do some file sharing, printing and maybe an Intranet page or three is far cheaper than using Windows. At very long last, Microsoft has admitted as much. Of course, they're quick to point out that they have much more to offer than Linux, which may indeed be true. But at the end of the day, when you compare donuts to donuts and like functions against one another, Linux is cheaper. For a shop of Windows-trained staff, dealing with Linux will certainly be a challenge, requiring additional training, suffer the odd blunder here and there, and be less forgiving when it comes to detailed configuration. On the same token, make a Linux whiz use Windows and see how quickly you'll be searching for a replacement. Cheap is in the budget of the beholder.Recommend It!
